textPlain: Michel H. Devoret, Chief Scientist at Google Quantum AI, and in April 2020 John M. Martinis resigned from Google after being reassigned to an advisory role. [0]Sounds like there was some politics shenanigans between them where Martinis was moved into a useless role and took the hint at the height of covid lockdown.[0] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Michel_Devoret

This award involved some clever engineering to set up quantum effects in a macroscopic system, but was there any new physics involved here?(Still better than last year's award which wasn't really physics at all!)
I remember being introduced to this research when reading a weird paper on the unexpected efficiency of photosynthesis, but now I can't find that paper. Anyone got any hints?

Maybe you can find the paper you ate looking for starting here?
 https://newscenter.lbl.gov/2025/07/08/how-plants-manage-ligh...Tip: this page links to further reading of older stuff.

why annoy credible scientists when there is an AI underlord available.
Unexpected Quantum Efficiency of Photosynthesis: cite papers -->perplexity.aihints galore.

For the papers about their measurements, you can look at the references in the technical press release https://www.nobelprize.org/uploads/2025/10/advanced-physicsp...Can't help you with "a weird paper on the unexpected efficiency of photosynthesis", try asking a biologist at your local university, or possibly an organic chemist.
Where did you get that quote from? It's not in the linked article.
Not in the immediate future. US scientists can and will find refuge in other countries for the time being. https://www.nature.com/articles/d41586-025-00938-y
